titleStmt.title: Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 47

div.p: To all sons of Holy Mother Church, Walter Young of Roxburgh sends greeting in the Lord: You should all know that I have granted and quitclaimed to Ranulf, archdeacon of St Andrews, and to his successors in perpetuity, Scoonie, and Balkaithley, and the land that lies on the south side of the city of St Andrews, between the burns, the lands which Walter of blessed memory, archdeacon of the same place, my uncle, held and possessed during his lifetime, which after his time I myself also had and possessed. I have also resigned to the aforesaid archdeacon, Ranulf, the whole right that I had in the aforesaid lands, either from gift, or from grant, or else from confirmation of Bishop Hugh of blessed memory, or my uncle Archdeacon Walter; and I returned the charters which I had concerning those lands to the same aforenamed Ranulf when I knew them to belong to the archdeaconry of St Andrews; and so that this grant and resignation be established and firm for the future, I have reinforced them by the application of the seals of the lord R(alph), bishop of Brechin, and H(enry), abbot of Arbroath, and mine, hanging from the present page, wishing faith to be had in its wording and content in the future. With these witnesses: R(alph), bishop of Brechin; H(enry), abbot of Arbroath, and the religious community of the same place; ... archdeacon of Brechin; William and Peter, chaplain of the lord [bishop] of Brechin; Henry, chaplain of Montrose; Hervey, parson of Inchbraoch; Alan, clerk of Montrose; Roger of Inverkeithing; Herbert of Montrose; William son of Áed; Stephen the Mair; Robert of Hailes; and many others.
